compressed_prompt: write song lyrics on socio-economic issue, all lowercase, no commas
response:
# **paper walls**

verse 1
born on the wrong side of the zip code
mama working doubles just to keep the lights on
daddy never made it past the factory floor
they shut it down in ninety-three and locked the door
the school got no books but the prison got beds
they feeding us numbers but we counting the dead

chorus
we building your towers with our broken hands
we growing your table but we eating off cans
you drawing the lines and we living between
the richest damn country that the world's ever seen
yeah the richest damn country that the world's ever seen

verse 2
little girl with a dream and a library card
the loans came easy but the interest hit hard
ten years of working just to break even now
the bootstraps they sold her already snapped somehow
the landlord raised the rent on a tuesday night
she's three checks from the street and she's losing the fight

chorus
we building your towers with our broken hands
we growing your table but we eating off cans
you drawing the lines and we living between
the richest damn country that the world's ever seen

bridge
this ain't about lazy

this is about who gets to sit
at the front of the gate
the zip code you're born in

outro
